requirements.education: Bachelor's degree or higher in specialty field (or equivalent experience)
requirements.other: Subject to annual lottery cap; Premium processing available for $2805

rejection_risk_factors: Degree not directly related to job specialty
Specialty occupation status not clearly established
Cap-exempt employer not available (universities are cap-exempt)
eligibility_text: Applicant must have a job offer in a specialty occupation requiring theoretical and practical application of a body of specialized knowledge. Must hold at least a bachelor's degree or equivalent in the specific specialty. Employer must be a US entity willing to file H-1B petition with USCIS. Subject to annual cap of 65,000 regular + 20,000 US Master's exemption. Selected via lottery if petitions exceed cap.
next_steps: Find a sponsoring employer in the US
Employer files LCA with Department of Labor
Employer files I-129 petition with USCIS
Wait for lottery result (if cap-subject)
Attend visa interview at US consulate

requirements.other: Must demonstrate extraordinary ability via awards, publications, high salary, media coverage, judging, critical role, or original contributions

rejection_risk_factors: Awards not nationally or internationally recognized
Publications not in major peer-reviewed journals
eligibility_text: For individuals with extraordinary ability in sciences, education, business, or athletics. Must meet 3 of 8 criteria: awards, membership in associations, published material, judging work, original contributions, scholarly articles, critical role at distinguished organization, or high salary. No annual cap — no lottery.
next_steps: Document all achievements, awards, publications
Find US employer or agent willing to petition
Gather recommendation letters from experts
File I-129 with USCIS
No consular wait for cap like H-1B

requirements.education: High school diploma or equivalent for undergrad; Bachelor's for grad
requirements.other: Acceptance letter from SEVP-certified school, proof of financial support ($20,000-$60,000/year depending on school), I-20 form

rejection_risk_factors: Weak ties to home country (visa officer doubts return)
Language test score below university requirement
eligibility_text: Must be accepted at a SEVP-approved US school and receive Form I-20. Must prove sufficient financial resources for full tuition and living expenses. Must demonstrate strong ties to home country and intent to return after studies. Work authorization limited to on-campus and CPT/OPT.
next_steps: Apply and get accepted to a US university
Receive I-20 from school's DSO
Pay SEVIS fee ($350)
Apply for F-1 visa at US embassy
Attend visa interview

requirements.other: Minimum 67/100 FSW points. CRS score determines invitation. Recent draws: CRS 470-530 range.

rejection_risk_factors: CRS score below current cutoff (check latest draw)
Work experience not in NOC TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3
Provincial job offer could boost CRS significantly
eligibility_text: Points-based system using Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS). Scored on age (max 110), education (max 150), work experience (max 80), language (max 160), adaptability (max 100), and job offer (50/200 extra). Must meet minimum 67/100 FSW points. Invited through regular draws targeting specific NOC categories.
next_steps: Take IELTS or CELPIP language test
Get Educational Credential Assessment (WES/ICES)
Create Express Entry profile
Calculate your CRS score
Wait for Invitation to Apply (ITA) draw
Submit PR application within 60 days of ITA

requirements.other: Acceptance letter from DLI, proof of CAD $10,000+ per year for living expenses, no criminal record

eligibility_text: Must be accepted by a Designated Learning Institution (DLI). Must show proof of financial support (tuition + CAD $10,000 living/year). Work up to 20 hrs/week on campus. PGWP after graduation leads directly to Express Entry pathway.
next_steps: Apply to a DLI in Canada
Receive acceptance letter
Apply for study permit online or at embassy
Provide biometrics
Apply for PGWP after graduation for work rights
alternative_visas: Express Entry after graduation via PGWP

requirements.education: No specific degree, but role must be at required skill level
requirements.other: Must score 70 points: job offer (20) + skill level (20) + English (10) + salary (20). Salary threshold is GBP 38,700 (or going rate for occupation, whichever higher).

rejection_risk_factors: Employer not on Home Office sponsor register
Criminal record with sentence over 12 months
eligibility_text: Points-based visa requiring 70 points. Job offer from licensed sponsor (20pts), role at required skill level RQF 3+ (20pts), English language (10pts), salary at/above threshold (20pts). No annual quota. Fast processing (~3 weeks). ILR possible after 5 years.
next_steps: Find UK employer on sponsor register
Receive Certificate of Sponsorship (CoS)
Apply online for Skilled Worker visa
Prove English language ability
Provide biometrics at visa centre

requirements.education: UK Bachelor's, Master's or PhD from approved provider
requirements.other: Must have studied in UK on Student visa for full course duration. PhD graduates get 3 years instead of 2.

rejection_risk_factors: Didn't complete full course in UK
eligibility_text: 2-year (3 for PhD) open work visa for UK university graduates. No job offer needed. No salary threshold. Work in any role, switch to Skilled Worker without leaving UK. Highly valued by international students as stepping stone to ILR.
next_steps: Graduate from approved UK university
Apply within 2 years of graduation
No job offer needed — work freely
Find sponsored role to switch to Skilled Worker

requirements.education: German-recognized university degree or vocational qualification
requirements.language: German A1+ (B2 preferred); English for IT roles
requirements.other: Must show proof of ~EUR 5,000 for living costs during 6-month search period. Cannot work during this visa — only job search.

rejection_risk_factors: Degree not recognized by Germany (use Anabin database)
German language skills lacking (except IT sector)
eligibility_text: 6-month visa to search for a job in Germany. Must hold a German-recognized degree. Upon finding a job, convert to EU Blue Card or work permit without leaving Germany. Germany has massive shortage of IT, engineering, and healthcare workers.
next_steps: Get degree recognition via anabin.kmk.org
Open German bank account or show sufficient funds
Apply at German consulate in home country
Travel to Germany and actively job search
Convert to EU Blue Card once job is found

requirements.language: German B1 for PR; English sufficient for most IT roles
requirements.other: Minimum gross salary: EUR 45,300 (2024); IT/engineering/math shortage occupations: EUR 41,041. PR possible in 21 months with B1 German.

eligibility_text: EU-wide work permit for highly qualified non-EU nationals. Requires recognized degree and job offer meeting salary thresholds. PR (Niederlassungserlaubnis) possible in 21 months with B1 German or 33 months without. Family reunification within months. Path to German citizenship in 5-8 years.
next_steps: Secure job offer with salary above threshold
Apply at German embassy or immigration office
Provide degree recognition
Arrive and register (Anmeldung) at local Bürgeramt
Start German language classes for faster PR
alternative_visas: Job Seeker Visa → EU Blue Card

requirements.education: Skills assessment by relevant authority (e.g. ACS for IT)
requirements.other: Points test: 65+ points. Under 45 years of age. Occupation on MLTSSL. Recent invitations: 85-90 points for competitive occupations.

rejection_risk_factors: Occupation not on Medium and Long-term Strategic Skills List
Points score below current invitation round
Skills assessment not approved (ACS for IT — often rejected)
eligibility_text: Permanent residency visa with no employer or state sponsor required. Points scored on age (max 30 for 25-32), English (max 20), overseas experience (max 15), Australian experience (max 20), education (max 20), specialist education (max 10), and various bonuses. Must be invited via SkillSelect after submitting EOI.
next_steps: Get skills assessed by the relevant authority
Take IELTS for Competent or higher English
Submit Expression of Interest (EOI) in SkillSelect
Receive invitation when points score is competitive
Apply for visa within 60 days of invitation

requirements.education: Prior educational qualifications matching course level
requirements.other: CoE from registered education provider, GTE requirement (Genuine Temporary Entrant), AUD 29,710+ in savings, OSHC insurance

eligibility_text: Covers courses from primary school to PhD. Work rights: 48 hours/fortnight during term, unlimited during breaks. Graduates can apply for Temporary Graduate Visa (Subclass 485) — 2-6 years work rights depending on qualification and location.
next_steps: Apply to registered Australian institution
Receive Confirmation of Enrolment (CoE)
Write Genuine Temporary Entrant statement
Apply online for subclass 500
Get OSHC insurance

requirements.other: Minimum SGD 5,000/month (higher for older/more experienced). Evaluated via COMPASS (Complementarity Assessment Framework) from 2023. Points on salary, qualifications, diversity, support for locals.

eligibility_text: For foreign professionals, managers, executives, and specialists. Evaluated using COMPASS scoring from Sep 2023. Points for: salary vs peers (0-20), qualifications (0-20), diversity (0-20), support for local employment (0-20), skills bonus (10-20). Minimum 40 points. PR possible after 2+ years.
next_steps: Receive job offer from Singapore employer
Employer applies via EP Online
Approval typically in 3 weeks
Collect EP card after arrival
Apply for Dependant Pass for family

requirements.other: Multiple pathways: (1) Investors: AED 2M real estate or AED 500K business; (2) Entrepreneurs: approved startup; (3) Specialized talent: doctors, engineers, scientists, artists; (4) Outstanding graduates; (5) Humanitarian workers

rejection_risk_factors: University not ranked in top 500 globally
eligibility_text: 10-year renewable residency. No sponsor required after issuance. Sponsor your own family. No minimum stay requirement. Tax-free income. Multiple categories: investors (AED 2M property/AED 500K business), entrepreneurs, specialized talents (scientists, doctors, engineers, artists, athletes), outstanding students (top 5% or GPA 3.75+), humanitarian workers.
next_steps: Identify which Golden Visa category you qualify for
Apply via ICP website or GDRFA
Medical examination
Emirates ID registration
Renew every 10 years

requirements.education: No specific requirement but must meet salary threshold
requirements.language: Dutch not required; English widely spoken
requirements.other: Minimum gross monthly salary EUR 5,331 (under 30: EUR 3,909). Employer must be IND-recognized sponsor. 30% tax ruling (tax-free allowance) often available to expats.

eligibility_text: Fast-track work permit for highly skilled workers. No quota. Processing in 2 weeks with recognized sponsor. 30% ruling saves significant income tax for first 5 years. After 5 years, apply for Dutch permanent residence (PR). Gateway to EU Blue Card and Schengen area freedom.
next_steps: Find employer with IND recognition (most multinationals qualify)
Employer submits application to IND
MVV (entry visa) issued if needed
Collect residence permit at IND desk
Register at local municipality (BRP)

requirements.education: Relevant degree for occupations on Critical Skills list
requirements.job_offer: Required — EUR 38,000+ salary (EUR 32,000 for some shortage roles)
requirements.other: Occupation must be on Critical Skills Occupations List. After 2 years: Stamp 4 (work without permit). After 5 years: Irish citizenship pathway.

rejection_risk_factors: Occupation not on Critical Skills list
eligibility_text: Fast-track permit for in-demand occupations. IT, engineering, healthcare, finance are all eligible. After 2 years, Stamp 4 allows work for any employer without permit. Spouse can work freely on Stamp 1G. Irish citizenship in 5 years. English-speaking, EU member, US tech hub.
next_steps: Verify occupation on Critical Skills list
Secure job offer with qualifying salary
Employer applies for permit
Apply for entry visa (if required)
Register with GNIB on arrival

requirements.education: No formal requirement — job offer is primary criterion
requirements.language: Swedish not required initially; English widely used in tech
requirements.other: Salary must be equivalent to Swedish collective agreement (usually SEK 27,360+/month for IT). Union (fackförbund) must be offered opportunity to comment.

eligibility_text: Sweden's employer-sponsored work permit is one of Europe's most open systems — no quota, no skills list requirement, any occupation. Employer must offer terms matching Swedish collective agreements. After 4 years, permanent residency. Strong social benefits, family included.
next_steps: Get a job offer from a Swedish employer
Employer advertises role to EU workers first (2 weeks)
Apply at Migrationsverket online
Decision in 2-4 months
Collect residence permit card in Sweden

requirements.education: Recognized qualification (assessed by NZQA if non-NZ)
requirements.other: Points-based EOI. 160+ points invited. Points for: skilled employment in NZ (max 50), skilled employment offer (max 50), qualifications (max 70), work experience (max 30), age (max 30). Under 56 years old.

rejection_risk_factors: Age over 56 at time of application
eligibility_text: Permanent residency via points-based system. Best if you have or can get a skilled NZ job offer — it's the biggest points booster. NZ Work Visa for 1-3 years is the typical pathway to get that offer first. Small, safe, English-speaking. Rural/regional bonus points available.
next_steps: Calculate your points at immigration.govt.nz
Consider NZ Work Visa first to gain local experience
Submit EOI when 160+ points achievable
Receive invitation to apply
Lodge resident visa application
